channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: A number of U.S. government web pages changed or went dark Friday as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Woman who wanted to kill GOP leaders said she was inspired by CEO's slaying, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors say a Massachusetts woman accused of planning to kill Republican leaders at the U.S. Capitol told police she was influenced by Luigi Mangione, the man charged with fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare last year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the NTSB, the agency investigating the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DC plane crash: Potomac River divers' search for bodies complicated by conditions out of their control
item.description: After an Army helicopter and American Airlines plane crashed into the Potomac River around Washington, D.C., a Virginia diver shared challenges of such a recovery effort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan boy, 5, killed in hyperbaric oxygen chamber explosion, officials say
item.description: A 5-year-old boy is dead after a hyperbaric chamber he was inside exploded at a medical facility Friday in Troy, Michigan, police and fire officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Robert Santos decides to resign as US Census Bureau director midway through a 5-year term
item.description: Robert Santos, who emphasized inclusivity and outreach to overlooked communities, has decided to resign as director of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina's ex-lieutenant governor dismisses lawsuit against CNN, has no plans to run again
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's former Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson on Friday withdrew his defamation lawsuit against CNN that challenged a report about him making explicit posts on a pornography website’s message board over a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man agrees to plead guilty for flying drone that damaged firefighting aircraft in LA wildfire
item.description: A man who launched a drone from the top of a parking structure and lost sight of it has agreed to plead guilty to unsafely operating a drone after it left a gaping hole in the wing of a firefighting aircraft during the deadly Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Department of Education undoes Biden's Title IX rules on gender
item.description: The Department of Education announced Friday it is scrapping former President Biden's 2024 Title IX changes, getting rid of discrimination protection based on gender identity and sexual orientation and changing how sexual assault cases are handled on campus.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Special Report: Behind the stories we uncovered for Black History Month
item.description: Among the topics discussed is a deeper dive into our coverage of a surge in black-owned businesses from WHTM's Valerie Pritchett, WFLA's Deanne King delving into stories of everyday people working to fix systemic problems in black communities, and KSEE's Sydney Charles reporting on the role Buffalo
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: ICT Together fund will help crash victims' families
item.description: The Wichita Foundation has started a fund to provide aid to help families of the American Airlines crash victims.
item.dc:creator: Laura McMillan, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights attorney from Wichita, Kansas, victim of plane crash in DC
item.description: A woman who made a name for herself in Wichita and was in the process of doing the same thing in Washington was on the plane that crashed in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Payton Steiner, NewsNation

item.title: Student, attorney among Ohioans killed in D.C. plane crash
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- A 20-year-old student at an Ohio university was among those killed Wednesday night when an American Airlines passenger jet collided with an Army helicopter in Washington D.C. Grace Maxwell was a junior at Cedarville University.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, NewsNation

item.title: Olympian Nancy Kerrigan mourns loss of skating community members after DC plane crash
item.description: “Much like everyone here has been saying is not sure how to process it,” she told MSNBC while in tears on Thursday at the Boston Skating Club.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans advance Trump OMB nominee as Democrats boycott vote
item.description: Senate Republicans advanced the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office as Democrats boycotted the meeting to protest the administration's recent efforts to freeze large swaths of federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: FDA approves painkiller designed to eliminate the risks of addiction associated with opioids
item.description: Federal officials on Thursday approved a new type of pain drug designed to eliminate the risks of addiction and overdose associated with opioid medications like Vicodin and OxyContin.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: Black Caucus blasts Trump's DEI comments after Potomac plane crash
item.description: "Not only are the President’s claims untrue, they also speak to the Republican Party’s desire to divide us as a country.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: A police officer testifies that an ex-prosecutor never obstructed her in the Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — The case against a former Georgia district attorney charged with discouraging police from arresting the man who killed Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 took a blow Thursday as a police supervisor testified that the accused prosecutor never obstructed her during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Staffing at Reagan Washington National Airport air control tower was 'not normal' on night of collision: FAA
item.description: The staffing at the air control tower at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport was "not normal" when an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter collided on Wednesday night, a report shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Midwest woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: "I am concerned that NPR and PBS broadcasts could be violating federal law by airing commercials," FCC Chair Brendan Carr wrote.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Google searches for 'my dog is depressed' skyrocket
item.description: Google searches for “my dog is depressed” have skyrocketed by 463% in the past month, indicating growing concern among pet owners.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, NewsNation

item.title: LISTEN: Audio captures air traffic control at moment of DC crash
item.description: Audio of air traffic control captured the moment that workers observed a Black Hawk helicopter colliding with a passenger jet near Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: What to know about military helicopter involved in crash near DC
item.description: A deadly collision on Wednesday night between an American Airlines plane with 60 passengers and four crew and an Army UH-60 Black Hawk with three soldiers remains under investigation amid questions about how the crash occurred.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KRON4

item.title: Missing Colorado juvenile found in Houston; Suspect arrested by Bellaire police
item.description: BELLAIRE, Texas (KIAH) – A missing juvenile from Colorado has been safely recovered in Houston, thanks to the swift action by the Bellaire Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Estrada, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about the DC plane crash victims
item.description: Among those killed were figure skaters, union members, and a couple en route to visit their child at college.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, Fox 5

item.title: Here are some of the deadliest plane crashes in US history
item.description: An American Airlines jet with 60 passengers and four crew members aboard collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while coming in for a landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, D.C. Everyone aboard was feared dead, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No survivors after collision between American Airlines jet, Army helicopter near DC
item.description: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members that collided with an Army helicopter was feared dead in what was likely to be the worst U.S. aviation disaster in almost a quarter century, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger jet with 64 aboard collides with Army helicopter while landing at Reagan Airport near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — An American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, Fox 5

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4
